Modules
한
Hangul & Reading
83 q

Learn the Korean alphabet — vowels, consonants, and how syllables combine.

안
Greetings & Politeness
42 q

Hello, thank you, sorry — and the formality levels behind them.

一
Numbers & Counting
52 q

Native and Sino-Korean numbers, plus counters for time, age, and money.

은
Particles
51 q

The system: 은/는, 이/가, 을/를, 에, 에서 — what each one marks and why.

하
Verbs & Conjugation
80 q

Dictionary form, polite -아/어요, formal -ㅂ니다, and past -았/었어요 across common verbs.

집
Daily Life Vocabulary
134 q

Food, family, places, time, objects, colors — everyday words in context.

문
Sentence Patterns
102 q

SOV order, the copula, negation, questions — building real sentences.

글
Reading & Translation
76 q

Conversation practice

Chat in Korean (Hangul) with a partner. When you're done, get feedback on what you wrote — meaning, grammar, particles and naturalness.

Start with a short, low-stakes chat

Pick the recommended scenario first, write 3-5 short replies, then end the chat. Feedback is generated after the conversation, so it may take a moment and should be treated as practice guidance, not native review.

Loading scenarios…

About Baeu

Baeu is a Korean practice app that turns short, mixed exercises into steady mastery through spaced review.

Who it's for

Self-directed learners working toward TOPIK 1 who want frequent, low-friction practice with honest progress signals — not a course.

What's there today

A handful of modules and published questions across translation, multiple choice, and fill-in-the-blank. The adaptive review queue resurfaces what you keep getting wrong, and the Progress page shows accuracy, skill mastery, weak tags, and recent activity.

For cohorts and sponsors

Baeu is currently best suited for a small, supervised learner cohort: a teacher, parent, or sponsor can ask learners to practice daily and review their Progress screen as a lightweight report. It is not yet a school LMS, placement test, certificate, or payment product.

Pedagogy scope

The curriculum is TOPIK 1-oriented and intentionally narrow: Hangul, beginner grammar, common vocabulary, particles, senten
